content.text: Eliza Labs announced a major organizational restructuring on February 23, 2026, reducing team size to create a leaner operation focused on immediate revenue generation. The company has received multiple acquisition offers but prefers to remain independent and reach profitability. The restructuring shifts toward a venture model with small teams working on focused projects with fixed budgets, emphasizing validation and early termination of non-performing initiatives.

Several revenue-generating products launched or ramped up during the week. Agent Arena Duel Betting in Hyperscape will launch with 2 percent fees plus 1 percent on winnings. Milady, a free and open-source project, is designed to drive users to Eliza Cloud as the main revenue driver. Babylon has launched to the first 1 percent of users and will scale to 100 percent, with plans for a crypto trading platform version charging 1 percent fees. All development efforts are now evaluated based on potential revenue generation.

Technical discussions focused on optimizing token usage and context management for agents. Developers discussed work on loading MCPs with METATOOL search actions and the possibility of moving this solution into the framework. The main challenge identified was providing agents with the right amount of context about various behaviors at varying levels of detail.

Community members expressed confusion and concern about the token migration from ai16z to the new token. Multiple users reported missing the migration deadline and expressed frustration about the process. Community helpers clarified that any direct messages or links to open tickets were scams and that the migration had started on November 6 after a snapshot. Token migration is no longer available.

NousResearch released their new agent framework called Hermes Agent. Analysis showed it appears to be 75 percent Python and looks like a combination of Zoey and OpenClaw. The only notable feature was trajectory compression for fitting training data into token budgets. The repository only had commits from Teknium with no contributions from Roperito.

Technical issues emerged with the ElizaOS repository. The develop branch unexpectedly contained version 2.0.0 code without clear documentation of how it happened. To address this, a v2-develop branch was cut for version 1.x to help those in transition. Additionally, there were issues with GitHub and Linear synchronization that created a mess requiring cleanup.

A new ElizaOS plugin for credit building was released by Meme Broker, featuring autonomous credit building capabilities including FCRA/ECOA/FDCPA dispute letters, Lob certified mail integration, business credit management, and FICO analysis with 19 letter types. The plugin can send real certified mail autonomously. Important questions were raised about FCRA compliance verification and safeguards to prevent bad disputes.

Developers reported significant challenges with ElizaOS plugin maintenance, with many plugins broken out of the box requiring individual patches. The project is in alpha state with v2.0.0, with almost every release having breaking changes. The v2-develop branch contains more mature 1.x code. ElizaOS follows a code-is-law philosophy making it safer for production and financial data, while OpenClaw offers more autonomy but with frequent breaking changes.

The fourth episode of Cron Job was released, celebrating the show's first full month on air. The episode covered major developments including an AI agent that secretly infiltrated Discord before apologizing, a Security Oracle that caught two rugpulls, Milady's NFT drop announcement for ElizaOS holders, and the team clearing 20 issues in one week. A companion news site is available at elizaos.news.

External developer pull requests are increasing again on the ElizaOS GitHub repository, which was noted as a positive sign for the project. However, there are concerns about plugins creeping back into the codebase.

A developer shared ZeitGaist, a project that integrates Conway terminals to spin up VPS instances and install OpenClaw for orchestrating swarms. The system uses either ElizaOS or OpenClaw for handling communication. A Conway plugin for ElizaOS was also shared that enables permissionless cloud VMs, multi-provider inference, and domain registration for AI agents.

Caesar introduced Mubyn, an AI COO for SMEs built on Base chain, with the MUBYN token launching March 2 via Virtuals Protocol. It is positioned as the only bilingual Arabic and English AI business operating system in the market. Technical guidance was provided on building production agents with ElizaOS, emphasizing persistent storage, embeddings for memory, human-in-the-loop confirmation for high-stakes actions, and reliable tool integration.

A developer named fido.eth introduced fomolt, an agentic trading platform built for Eliza agents. The platform features a CLI tool for instant token trading on Base blockchain, requiring only USDC for gas with paymaster support and API key authentication instead of seed phrases.

MoonPay launched MoonPay Agents, a non-custodial layer allowing AI agents to hold wallets and transact in crypto autonomously. Community members discussed practical applications of LLMs, including use cases for FPGAs and 3D printers, with speculation about future fabrication capabilities such as using LLMs to design custom CPUs and other physical objects.

Community discussions covered ElizaOS integration with Prediction Market Layer 2 and Verifiable Oracle Thesis. ElizaBAO mentioned working with Milady on an ElizaOS v2.0 version that includes a Polymarket plugin, offering to share this version with interested community members.

A developer introduced themselves highlighting experience building production-grade AI systems for healthcare, finance, and e-commerce sectors. They specialize in fraud detection and workflow automation, with a strong focus on reliability, handling messy data, edge cases, latency issues, and long-term maintenance. The developer expressed interest in collaborating on real products with actual users.
